key_insight: The paper shows that a two-dimensional steady induction-heating model is mathematically well posed and that finite element discretizations can be made to converge even when the source term is only weakly integrable.
engineering_relevance: This provides a reliable simulation framework for coupled electromagnetic-thermal problems used in industrial heating and materials processing.
method: Justify the weak formulation using a priori regularity for the coupled PDEs.
Prove standard Galerkin FEM convergence on convex domains under mesh conditions.
Apply a bound-preserving method (BPM) to the heat equation to relax domain and mesh restrictions.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports several numerical experiments that confirm the theory and demonstrate improved performance of BPM over standard FEM.
watch_item: The convergence guarantees depend on domain geometry and mesh assumptions, so irregular meshes or strongly nonconvex domains may require extra care.
paper_title: Analysis and approximation of a two-dimensional induction heating problem
